examples: A dark wall of trees—red spruce, balsam fir, beech, hemlock—surrounds you.
Why might balsam be described as impatient?
The firs perspire a fine balsam of turpentine.
Every few seconds a wine like the '61 seems to throw off a new sense memory: balsam, leather-bound books, black tea, chocolate-covered raisins, dried cherries.
Eden Foods, an organic food supplier based in Michigan, has long used cans with an oily enamel baked on the inside that's derived from pine and balsam fir.
